Title: EPF bolsters retirement savings with new initiatives
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Employees Provident Fund (EPF) has introduced new initiatives to strengthen retirement security across generations including i-Legasi, i-Emas and a Retirement Goal Calculator.
EPF chief executive officer Ahmad Zulqarnain Onn said the initiatives reflect a shift in retirement planning from simply accumulating savings to ensuring long-term adequacy and sustainability.
"As life expectancy rises, retirement planning must go beyond savings accumulation to focus on adequacy and sustainability.
"We are evolving to support our members not just in saving, but in making informed decisions to ensure their savings last a lifetime and to leave a legacy for their loved ones," he said.
Through i-Legasi, EPF members aged 55 or 60 and above can transfer a portion of their excess retirement savings to immediate family members such as spouses and children.
"The transfer will come from savings that are already available for withdrawal and will be placed into the recipient's retirement savings accounts, which are meant for long-term savings.
"The facility transfers savings from the transferor's Akaun 55, or Akaun Emas for members aged 60 and above, to the transferee's Akaun Persaraan, or Akaun Emas for transferees aged 55 to 59, to ensure the savings remain preserved for retirement," EPF said.
Only savings above the adequate savings threshold are eligible for transfer and the funds will remain within EPF accounts for long-term retirement use.
Meanwhile, the monthly withdrawal option under the Age 55/60 Withdrawal has been rebranded as i-Emas.
The option encourages retirees to take structured monthly payments instead of withdrawing their savings in full, helping them manage expenses while ensuring a steady income stream and continued dividend earnings.
EPF also launched the Retirement Goal Calculator via the KWSP i-Akaun app.
The tool helps members estimate their retirement needs based on lifestyle expectations and projected expenses, identify savings gaps and plan more effectively for the future.
The EPF said the initiatives form part of a broader effort to build a more holistic retirement system that supports financial resilience not just for individuals, but across generations.
"Our goal is to help our members retire with confidence and dignity. By strengthening the financial resilience of the next generation through i-Legasi and encouraging sustainable habits with i-Emas, we are building a more secure future for our members," Ahmad Zulqarnain said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: ACE Market-bound MM Computer Systems aims to RM26.18mil from IPO
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Information technology (IT) solutions provider MM Computer Systems Bhd (MMCS) aims to raise RM26.18 million from its initial public offering (IPO) via its listing on the ACE Market of Bursa Malaysia Securities Bhd on June 11, 2026.
The IPO comprises a public issue of 119 million new shares priced at 22 sen per share, alongside an offer for sale of 47.34 million existing shares.
MMCS said the proceeds will primarily be used to support the expansion of the group's project execution capabilities, including the procurement of IT hardware and software, workforce expansion, capability development and partial repayment of bank borrowings.
Managing director and chief executive officer Young Yoong Chang said enterprise IT environments are becoming increasingly complex and security-sensitive.
He said MMCS has been building its capabilities in IT infrastructure, networking, cybersecurity and IT outsourcing services over the past two decades to meet evolving industry requirements.
"As enterprise digitalisation and cybersecurity requirements continue to evolve, we believe MMCS is well-positioned to support increasingly sophisticated IT environments across both the public and private sectors," he said at the launch of the company's prospectus here today.
During a press conference after launch, Young said MMCS expects annual growth of about 20 per cent in both revenue and profit over the next two years.
He added that approximately RM37 million from the group's order book is expected to be recognised in the financial year ending 2026, while the remaining balance would be recognised progressively through multi-year contracts running until 2029, depending on project delivery timelines.
Meanwhile, regarding the ongoing United States-Iran situation, head of business development Quah Soo Keat said higher hardware prices and inflationary pressures could affect operating costs.
"It may affect our costs, particularly in terms of hardware pricing, but our margins remain manageable," he said.
Quah added that the company is able to pass on part of the higher costs to customers.
"Our customers understand the current market conditions, so the impact on our business is relatively manageable," he said.
Operating through its wholly owned subsidiaries, Micro Technology Solution Sdn Bhd (MTS) and SMIND Sdn Bhd, MMCS provides enterprise IT infrastructure, networking and cybersecurity solutions for mission-critical operations.
Applications for the IPO open today and close on May 25, 2026, at 5pm.
Malacca Securities Sdn Bhd is appointed as the principal adviser, sponsor, underwriter and placement agent for the IPO exercise, whereas SCS Global Advisory (M) Sdn Bhd is appointed as the corporate finance adviser.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Gold falls on oil-driven inflation worries as US–Iran peace talks falter
Article:
NEW YORK: Gold prices fell on Monday, as a lack of progress in US–Iran peace negotiations pushed oil prices higher, fuelling concerns that elevated inflation could keep interest rates higher for longer.
US President Donald Trump on Sunday rejected Iran's response to a US proposal for peace talks, dashing hopes for an imminent end to the 10-week-old conflict that has paralysed maritime traffic in the Strait of Hormuz, and driven up global energy prices.
Spot gold fell 0.8 per cent at US$4,676.02 per ounce, as of 0726 GMT. US gold futures for June delivery lost one per cent at US$4,684.50.
"We're essentially seeing an unwinding of hopes for an imminent (peace) deal, and gold is feeling the pinch from the renewed rise in crude prices," said Tim Waterer, chief market analyst at KCM Trade.
Brent oil jumped four per cent to US$105 a barrel, while the dollar rose, making greenback-priced bullion more expensive for holders of other currencies.
While gold is traditionally seen as a hedge against inflation, high interest rates tend to weigh on the non-yielding asset.
The ongoing war with Iran and its shock to oil prices and supplies have rocketed to the top of the list of concerns for financial stability, according to a semi-annual Federal Reserve report released on Friday.
Goldman Sachs pushed back its forecast of Fed rate cuts to December 2026 and March 2027 from its previous outlook of cuts in September and December this year.
Investors are now looking out for April's US Consumer Price Index data, due later this week, for further clues on the Fed's monetary policy direction.
Meanwhile, China's gold production fell in the first quarter of 2026 from a year earlier, the China Gold Association said on Saturday, as safety inspections led some smelters to suspend production for maintenance.
"In the near-to-medium term, the US$4,400 to US$4,800 range still looks firmly in play while we remain in this ceasefire-without-a-peace-deal stalemate," Waterer added.
Spot silver rose 0.3 per cent at US$80.57 per ounce, platinum slid 1.3 per cent to US$2,029.35, and palladium was down 1.2 per cent at US$1,473.30.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: King graces photo session for Umno's 80th anniversary at Istana Besar
Article:
JOHOR BARU: His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, today graced a photography session at Tangga Selat, Istana Besar Johor, in conjunction with Umno's 80th anniversary celebration.
According to a post on His Majesty's official Facebook page, accompanying the King was Tunku Ismail Sultan Ibrahim, the Regent of Johor.
The royal arrival was received by Umno president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi and Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi.
The photography session was held to commemorate the founding of Umno on May 11, 1946, at Istana Besar during the reign of Almarhum Sultan Sir Ibrahim.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Plan to trim T20 fuel subsidies 'disheartening'; MP warns of ripple effect
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: A government proposal to review fuel subsidies for the T20 income group has sparked concern from an opposition lawmaker.
Permatang Pauh member of parliament Muhammad Fawwaz Mohamad Jan warned that the move could ultimately burden lower-income Malaysians through a chain reaction of higher prices.
He said the proposal was "disheartening" and risks undermining the broader structure of consumer pricing in the country if implemented without careful safeguards.
He said fuel subsidies should remain a shared national benefit rather than being segmented strictly by income class.
"Fuel subsidies should be enjoyed by all Malaysians, regardless of income level.
"For the poor, it is a basic right that must be protected. For the rich, it reflects a return on their contribution through higher taxation," he told the
New Straits Times
.
Yesterday, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim had said the proposal for
more comprehensive targeted subsidies
, particularly the rationalisation of RON95 fuel subsidies for the T20 income group, was still being refined and expected to be finalised soon.
He had said the proposal was currently being reviewed by the Crisis Management Task Force under the National Economic Action Council (NEAC), chaired by Tan Sri Mohd Hassan Marican.
Economists had previously estimated that excluding the T20 group from the Budi95 subsidy programme could save the government up to RM1.5 billion a month.
Fawwaz, however, warned that targeting the T20 group could have unintended consequences for the wider economy.
"When higher costs are imposed on the T20, those costs will not remain at that level.
"They will be passed down through goods and services. In the end, it is the ordinary people who will feel the impact through higher prices," he added.
Fawwaz argued that the policy reflected a structural weakness in the current subsidy and tax framework, which he said needed to be addressed more holistically rather than through piecemeal cuts.
Instead of reducing subsidies, he urged the government to explore alternative revenue streams targeting high-income earners and large corporations.
Among his proposals were the introduction of a tax on large corporate or individual savings holdings, and a 2.5 per cent tax on share trading activity.
He said share transactions in Malaysia exceeded RM2 trillion in 2024, yet remain untaxed, despite being concentrated among wealthier investors.
Such measures, he said, could help sustain fuel subsidies without shifting the burden onto consumers, as the cost would not be directly passed on to end users in the same way as consumption-based taxes.
Fawwaz maintained his position that fuel subsidies should be preserved, but financed more equitably through diversified taxation rather than cuts that risk widening economic pressure on the broader population.
"The subsidy should not be withdrawn. The government must instead look for fairer and more sustainable sources of revenue," he noted.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Ukraine reports Russian attacks and battlefield clashes despite ceasefire
Article:
KYIV: Ukraine's military and regional officials on Monday reported  Russian drone strikes and battlefield clashes over the past 24 hours, despite a US-brokered ceasefire.
Ukraine and Russia agreed on Friday to a ceasefire from May 9 to May 11 as part of a US-led push for peace under President Donald Trump to end more than four years of war since Russia's full-scale invasion in 2022.
The ceasefire, which Trump said on Friday he hoped would be extended, was already showing signs of strain on Sunday, when each side accused the other of violating it.
Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskiy said on Sunday Moscow had refrained from large-scale aerial and missile attacks but that it had continued assaults along parts of the long front where Russian forces are advancing.
Russia's Defence Ministry accused Ukraine on Sunday of flouting the ceasefire, saying it had downed 57 Ukrainian drones over the past day and "responded in kind" on the battlefield.
It did not immediately report on the battlefield situation on Monday.
In Ukraine, one person was killed and two people were wounded in the southeastern region of Zaporizhzhia, the regional governor said.
Two were killed and two wounded in the southern region of Kherson, the Kherson region's governor said.
Three people were wounded in the neighbouring Mykolaiv region, five were hurt in the northern region of Kharkiv and four were wounded in the eastern region of Donetsk, according to reports by the regions' governors.
The General Staff of Ukraine's military said 180 battlefield clashes had been recorded along the front line over the past 24 hours and that Russian forces had on Sunday deployed 8,037 "kamikaze" drones in attacks on settlements and military positions. — REUTERS
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: BSN names Mujibburrahman Abd Rashid acting CEO as Jay Khairil moves to KWAP
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bank Simpanan Nasional (BSN) has appointed its deputy chief executive Mujibburrahman Abd Rashid as acting chief executive following the departure of Datuk Jay Khairil.
In a statement, BSN said that Mujibburrahman has served the bank since 2018 and brings extensive experience in banking operations, management and business.
The bank also confirmed that Jay Khairil will transition from his role as BSN chief executive to become Kumpulan Wang Persaraan (Diperbadankan) (KWAP) chief executive officer, effective May 20.
BSN said that Jay Khairil has served the organisation since February 2021 and has more than 30 years of experience in the banking and financial services sector.
Its chairman, Datuk Seri Dr Awang Adek Hussin, said Jay Khairil has been a key driver in strengthening the bank's financial performance and social mandate, while also reinforcing governance and broadening financial inclusion, positioning the bank as a sustainable and resilient financial institution.
BSN said his leadership strengthened the bank's delivery of its mandate, refined its strategic direction and enhanced trust among customers.
The bank's financial performance reflected this progress, with fee-based income growing at a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 8.4 per cent, while financing expanded at a CAGR of 8.1 per cent.
The stability of retail deposits and diversification of income sources also strengthened the bank's foundation for sustainable growth.
Jay Khairil's proactive and consistent approach positioned BSN as the nation's leading microfinance provider through the implementation of high-impact initiatives.
More than 72,000 micro and small entrepreneurs received financing totalling RM2.2 billion, giving BSN a 66 per cent market share in the segment.
On the digital front, nearly 50 per cent of BSN customers have migrated to the myBSN platform, with more than 75 per cent of banking transactions now conducted digitally.
He also played a key role in supporting the government's agenda through the nationwide distribution of financial assistance.
BSN further strengthened its financial inclusion mandate by expanding banking access through its branch network, BSN Agent Bank and Mobile Branch Service to ensure financial services remain inclusive and accessible to the public.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 'I only have RM5,000': Man charged with robbing aunt pleads for lower bail
Article:
BATU PAHAT: A factory worker pleaded not guilty at the Sessions Court here today to robbing his aunt of a gold bracelet worth RM15,000 last month.
Nor Anuar Jabar, 35, made a plea for lower bail, claiming he could only afford to pay RM5,000.
"I only have RM5,000", the father of three said during mitigation.
Judge Osman Affendi Mohd Shalleh set bail at RM10,000 and Anuar failed to post bail.
He was sent to the Muar Correctional Centre pending trial.
Anuar was charged with robbing his 72-year-old aunt at a house in Kampung Gombak, Simpang 5, at 1pm on April 20.
The charge was framed under Section 392 of the Penal Code, which carries a sentence of up to 14 years' jail and a fine or whipping upon conviction.
According to the police report, the victim was at home when she was approached by a masked man who snatched her gold bracelet.
She screamed for help and the masked man fled with the jewellery into a an oil palm plantation behind the house.
Following the report, police detained the suspect in Klang, Selangor.
Deputy public prosecutor Sharifah Natasha Syed Ahmad prosecuted while Anuar was unrepresented.
Should the accused post bail, the court ordered him to report to the nearest police station monthly, surrender his passport, and refrain from contacting the victim or prosecution witnesses.
The case is set for mention on June 8 for the submission of documents and the appointment of a defence lawyer.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Driver arrested after three-vehicle NKVE crash video goes viral [WATCH]
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Police have arrested a driver involved in a three-vehicle crash on the New Klang Valley Expressway (NKVE) heading towards the Duta Toll Plaza after a video of the incident went viral on social media.
Kuala Lumpur Traffic Investigation and Enforcement Department chief Assistant Commissioner Mohd Zamzuri Mohd Isa said the accident occurred at KM28 of the NKVE around 11pm on May 9.
He said the crash involved a Toyota Hilux, a Proton Wira and a Proton Inspira.
kejadian di highway menuju tol duta
hasanah_hk
pic.twitter.com/WwcZJ7A4kI
—  (@localrkyt)
May 10, 2026
"Initial investigations found that the Proton Inspira, driven by a 26-year-old local man, is believed to have skidded and come to a stop on the right lane of the highway.
"A Proton Wira driven by a 40-year-old Indonesian man then crashed into the vehicle before a Toyota Hilux driven by a 41-year-old local man also collided with the Proton Inspira.
"The impact caused both vehicles to veer to the left side of the road and crash into the road barrier," he said in a statement today.
Zamzuri said all three drivers escaped unhurt.
He said the Toyota Hilux and Proton Wira sustained damage in the incident.
"The extent of damage to the Proton Inspira is still under investigation," he said.
Zamzuri said police had tracked down and arrested the 26-year-old Proton Inspira driver to assist in investigations.
The case is being investigated under Section 42(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987 for reckless and dangerous driving.
Among the actions taken by police were obtaining CCTV recordings from the scene, recording witness statements, conducting a site inspection, and photographing the vehicles involved, he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Three youths held over viral 'wheelie' stunt in Lipis
Article:
LIPIS: Three youths believed to have performed dangerous "wheelie" stunts on their motorcycles in a video that went viral on Facebook were detained during a special operation here yesterday.
They were allegedly part of a group seen performing the stunts at Km4 Jalan Lipis-Felda Kechau, in front of a hotel, in a 22-second video posted on the "Komuniti Anak-Anak Lipis" Facebook page.
District police chief Superintendent Ismail Man said the video, captioned "meriah bandar Lipis ini" (Lipis town is lively) and uploaded on May 5, showed motorcyclists performing wheelie stunts.
"Acting on information, eight policemen conducted a special operation codenamed 'Op Samseng Jalanan' in Lipis town centre about 9pm yesterday and arrested three people.
"The suspects, aged 19 and 20, were arrested and their motorcycles seized. The motorcycles had been modified, and one did not even have rear brakes," he said in a statement today.
Ismail said the case is being investigated under Section 42(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987 for reckless and dangerous driving, which carries a maximum jail term of five years, a minimum fine of RM5,000, and the revocation of a driving licence or a two-year ban on applying for one.
He said police would intensify its crackdown on the "mat rempit" menace in the district and urged those with information to come forward so the culprits could be arrested.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Lorry driver charged with attempted murder in Bangsar shooting case
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A lorry driver pleaded not guilty in the Sessions Court here today to a charge of attempting to murder a man during a shooting incident outside an entertainment centre in Bangsar last month.
A. Lekeswaran, 27, was charged with attempting to kill scrap metal business owner B. Yoga Sundram, 41, in front of an entertainment outlet along Jalan Telawi, Bangsar, at about 3.15 am on April 22.
The charge, framed under Section 307 of the Penal Code, carries a maximum prison sentence of 10 years and a fine upon conviction.
If the act causes injury, the accused may face up to 20 years' imprisonment.
Deputy public prosecutor J. Banusha objected to bail, arguing that the offence was serious and noting that the victim remains in critical condition in the Intensive Care Unit of Universiti Malaya Medical Centre.
"Granting bail is inappropriate because it may allow the accused to flee, and there is also the possibility of interference with the victim and prosecution witnesses.
"It is also understood that the accused is expected to face firearms-related charges in the Klang Court.
"Therefore, I object to the bail application and request the court not to grant bail," he said.
Counsel Muhammad Izz Khan Mohd Nor Khan, representing the accused, appealed for bail and told the court that his client was willing to comply with any additional conditions imposed.
However, Judge Mazuliana Abdul Rashid denied bail and fixed June 10 for mention.– BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: US spending on operation against Iran tops US$77 billion
Article:
WASHINGTON: The cost of the US military operation against Iran continues to grow and has already exceeded US$77 billion by the 71st day of the operation, according to the Iran War Cost Tracker portal, reported Sputnik/RIA Novosti.
The resource updates data in real time and calculates the funds needed to maintain personnel, ships deployed to the region, and other related expenses.
The calculation methodology is based on a Pentagon report to Congress, which stated that the first six days cost US$11.3 billion, with costs expected to reach US$1 billion per day thereafter.
In late April, Under Secretary of Defence (Comptroller) and Chief Financial Officer Jules Hurst told lawmakers at a hearing of the House Armed Services Committee that US costs related to the conflict with Iran were approximately US$25 billion.
However, the very next day, US media reported citing informed sources that the stated amount did not include the cost of restoring American military facilities and replacing damaged equipment.
According to media reports, the actual costs were almost twice as high.
On Feb 28, the US and Israel began striking targets in Iran, killing over 3,000 people.
On April 8, Washington and Teheran declared a two-week ceasefire.
Subsequent talks in Islamabad ended inconclusively, with no resumption of hostilities reported, but the US began a blockade of Iranian ports. The ceasefire was extended.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Philippine lawmakers to vote on impeachment of presidential hopeful Sara Duterte
Article:
MANILA: Lawmakers in the Philippines are set to vote on Monday on whether to impeach Vice President Sara Duterte, in what could create a major hurdle for her bid to run for the presidency in 2028.
A house justice committee last month found probable cause for her impeachment in a petition from activists accusing her of misusing public funds, accumulating unexplained wealth and of threatening the lives of ⁠President Ferdinand Marcos Jr, his wife and the former house speaker.
With ally-turned-enemy Marcos limited by the constitution to a single term in office, Duterte is the clear favourite to succeed him in 2028. She has apologised for helping bring Marcos to power in 2022 after they ran on a joint ticket and won separate elections in landslides.
Duterte denies wrongdoing and her legal team has described the petition as defective and a "fishing expedition."
The scheduling of the vote for Monday was confirmed by House Secretary General Cheloy Garafil and representative Leila de Lima, a justice committee member.
If more than one third of the lower house backs her impeachment, the Senate will be required to convene a trial with its members as jurors. Duterte faces removal from office and a ban from politics if convicted.
DUTERTE FAMILY UNDER FIRE
The impeachment effort is the latest in a series of setbacks for the influential Duterte family, with the vice president feeling constant heat from her bitter feud with Marcos, and father Rodrigo Duterte awaiting trial at the International Criminal Court over a war on drugs that killed thousands of people during his 2016-2022 presidency.
The house last year voted to impeach Sara Duterte but that was later struck down by the Supreme Court over a procedural flaw.
She could become the highest-ranking official to be impeached in the Philippines after former President Joseph Estrada in 2000, whose trial was aborted after prosecutors walked out.
Three other top officials have been impeached, including an ombudsman and an election commission head, who both resigned before their trials, and former Supreme Court chief justice Renato Corona, the only one convicted so far.
Marcos survived a separate impeachment bid in February after his allies in Congress voted to dismiss it. He has distanced himself from proceedings against his estranged vice president.
Dozens of supporters of the impeachment effort gathered outside Congress on Monday, chanting and holding banners saying "Impeach Sara Now."
Among them was left-wing Congressman Perci Cendana, who said he was confident there would be enough votes to back it, calling it a "historic day for democracy." --REUTERS
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Turkish Airlines aircraft evacuated in Nepal after landing gear fire incident
Article:
KATHMANDU: A Turkish Airlines plane was evacuated after smoke was observed from its landing gear while it was taxiing after landing in Nepal's capital of Kathmandu, the airline said, forcing the closure of the airport for an hour.
The Civil Aviation Authority of Nepal (CAAN) had earlier said a fire broke out in the right rear tyre of the Airbus A330, which was doused before the plane was towed to the taxiway.
All 277 passengers and 11 crew on board were evacuated using the emergency exits, and nobody was injured, the aviation regulator and airline said.
"Technical inspections of the aircraft have been initiated by our teams. Initial assessments indicate that the smoke was caused by a technical malfunction in a hydraulic pipe," Yahya Ustun, the airline's senior vice president, communications, said on social media platform X.
An additional flight has been planned for the return of the passengers, he added.--REUTERS
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Mixue expands cold chain strategy amid rising F&B competition
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Food & beverage (F&B) companies such as Mixue Malaysia are beefing up their expansion and product quality strategy through a major cold chain upgrade initiative as competition intensifies in the country's fast-growing affordable premium beverage segment.
The company has begun gradually rolling out cold chain-enhanced products under its broader 2026 quality upgrade strategy, positioning Malaysia as one of its key Southeast Asian growth markets amid evolving consumer expectations and rising industry competition.
The initiative comes at a time when beverage operators are facing mounting pressure from consumers demanding fresher ingredients, higher-quality beverages and more consistent taste experiences, while also contending with rising operating costs, labour shortages and intensifying competition from both local and international brands.
"We are upgrading our fruit ingredients from room-temperature storage to a full cold chain system. All these efforts are ultimately aimed at delivering a product experience of real ingredients, fresh flavours and pure taste to consumers," said Teh Hwa Chun, central region area manager of Mixue Malaysia.
Under the upgrade, fruit ingredients are flash-frozen and distributed through a controlled cold chain system to preserve freshness and product consistency from source to store.
Analysts said the move reflects a broader shift within Malaysia's beverage and quick-service restaurant sector, where brands are increasingly investing in supply chain infrastructure and operational quality to differentiate themselves in a crowded market.
They noted that while cold chain investments improve product consistency and shelf life, they also increase operational complexity and cost pressures at a time when F&B operators are already grappling with rising rental expenses, higher wages, imported ingredient inflation and currency volatility.
At the same time, Mixue Malaysia has introduced an extensive nationwide franchisee training programme.
To support the rollout, Mixue's headquarters in China has deployed a professional training team to Malaysia to conduct specialised cold chain operational training for local franchisees and staff.
More than 10 professional trainers from China are providing on-site technical and operational support, enabling franchise operators to upgrade processes ranging from raw material handling and storage to beverage preparation and in-store execution.
Beyond operational upgrades, Mixue Malaysia said it remains focused on long-term localisation and community-driven growth strategies as it strengthens its footprint in the country.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: EU expected to agree on long-stalled sanctions on Israeli settlers: Kallas
Article:
BRUSSELS: The European Union was expected Monday to agree new sanctions on Israeli settlers over violence against Palestinians, the bloc's top diplomat said, as a change of government in Hungary ends months of blockage.
"I expect political agreement on the sanctions on violent settlers, hopefully we will get there," EU foreign policy chief Kaja Kallas said ahead of a meeting of the bloc's foreign ministers.
The move in response to rising violence and settlement expansion in the Israeli-occupied West Bank had been stalled by former Hungarian prime minister Viktor Orban.
But nationalist leader and Israel ally's ouster by rival Peter Magyar now appears to have paved the way for the veto to be lifted.
EU officials said seven settlers or settler organisations were set to be blacklisted.
The bloc was also set to sanction representatives from Palestinian resistancegroup Hamas.
The occupied West Bank has been gripped by almost daily violence since the start of the Gaza war in October 2023, involving both Israeli troops and settlers.
There has been a surge in deadly attacks by Israeli settlers in the occupied West Bank since the start of the Iran war on Feb 28, Palestinian officials and the United Nations have said.
While the EU is moving ahead with the sanctions on Israeli settlers, there remains no consensus among the bloc's member states to take further steps against Israel such as curbing trade ties.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Sg Juru pollution crisis threatens fishermen, shellfish farmers
Article:
JURU: About 100 shellfish farmers and fishermen in Kampung Kuala Juru here are growing increasingly alarmed over worsening pollution in Sungai Juru, which they say is destroying catches and threatening livelihoods built over generations.
The contamination has already caused income losses of more than 50 per cent following repeated deaths of farmed shellfish and fish in the river, which locals claim is heavily polluted by industrial effluent and wastewater from nearby aquaculture operations.
A recent monitoring visit by the Consumers' Association of Penang (CAP) found the river consistently muddy and discoloured, with strong indications of untreated discharge entering the waterway through factory outlets and drainage pipes linked to shrimp farms.
Kuala Juru Fishermen Association chairman Wahid Othman, 50, said the crisis had worsened over the past year, particularly after a waste discharge gate at Sungai Derhaka, which connects to Sungai Juru, was reportedly reopened after being closed for some time.
He said the reopening coincided with frequent pollution incidents, especially in the mornings when the river would often turn black and emit a strong stench.
"We have seen shellfish die in large numbers and even fish floating lifeless on the surface. It has never been this bad before," he said.
Wahid warned that continued pollution would devastate the local fishing economy and push many families into financial hardship.
"If this continues, the income of fishermen and shellfish farmers will keep dropping. Many families here depend entirely on the river," he added.
He called for immediate investigations and firm action against those responsible for the contamination.
CAP also voiced serious concern, urging the Penang government, the Department of Environment, the Department of Irrigation and Drainage, the Fisheries Department and local authorities to act without delay.
CAP president Mohideen Abdul Kader said urgent measures should include closing all suspected discharge points along Sungai Juru to prevent further pollution.
"This situation is not only an environmental crisis but also a growing threat to food security and the livelihoods of local communities," he said.
Mohideen also called for financial assistance to be extended to affected fishermen and shellfish farmers to help sustain their operations amid mounting losses.
Locals claimed Sungai Juru, once a vital source of income and sustenance, was now fast becoming a symbol of environmental decline, with urgent intervention needed before irreversible damage is done.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: RHB seals RM1bil financing deal for SMEs and mid-tier companies
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: RHB Banking Group has inked a strategic partnership with Syarikat Jaminan Pembiayaan Perniagaan Bhd (SJPP), a wholly‑owned entity of the Minister of Finance Inc, to provide up to RM1 billion in financing for small and medium enterprises (SMEs) and mid‑tier companies (MTCs).
In a statement, RHB said the initiative is part of broader efforts to support businesses facing challenges stemming from the ongoing Middle East conflict, while also strengthening access to funding for viable companies.
The collaboration aims to widen financing by lowering barriers to funding and supporting a broader base of SMEs. Eligible SMEs will also be able to benefit from restructuring, rescheduling and repayment assistance, enabling them to better manage cash flow pressures and sustain operations amid rising costs and a more challenging operating environment.
RHB said the RM1 billion allocation is earmarked under SJPP-administered government guarantee schemes, reinforcing its role as a participating financial institution under Bank Negara Malaysia's SME Stabilisation Relief Facility (SME SRF). The initiative complements national efforts to sustain business continuity during periods of heightened uncertainty.
Datuk Mohd Rashid Mohamad, group managing director/group chief executive officer of RHB Banking Group, said, "Through our partnership with SJPP and participation in SME SRF, we are committed to helping viable SMEs and MTCs access financing and navigate current challenges with greater resilience."
To improve outreach, RHB and SJPP will jointly conduct roadshows across key regions, including the Klang Valley, Johor Baru and Penang. These sessions will raise awareness of available financing solutions and facilitate direct engagement with SMEs, connecting eligible businesses to relevant programmes.
Juanita Rusmini Abdul Jalil, acting principal officer of SJPP, said that SJPP's collaboration with RHB strengthens the delivery of government guarantee schemes and expands financing support for viable businesses.
"By working closely with financial institutions, we are able to ensure that these programmes reach more SMEs, enabling them to sustain operations and move forward," Juanita said.
To date, RHB has supported more than 12,000 SMEs, reflecting the banking group's continued commitment to strengthening SME growth and resilience in Malaysia.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Old car plate numbers win Selangor man RM14.8 million jackpot
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A 52-year-old man won the Toto 4D Jackpot 1 worth RM14.8 million from the total RM16.8 million jackpot on May 6 by betting on his old car registration numbers.
The property manager from Selangor bet on the same pair of winning numbers - 9186 and 4828 - derived from the registration numbers of his old cars, which he said carried "sentimental value."
"I have been betting on these numbers for over 10 years because they remind me of my old cars and the memories attached to them.
"I never expected both numbers would strike in the same draw," he said.
The man said he would frequently read newspaper reports about Toto Jackpot winners and had often wondered whether such stories were truly genuine.
"Now that I have personally experienced it, I can say the winning stories are indeed real," he said.
He said he placed a total bet of RM6, which earned him RM14,818,901.35 and a bonus of RM336 at the STM Lottery head office.
He aims to spend his winnings on a luxury car and invest in properties. However, he said he would ensure his prize money is managed prudently for long-term financial security.
Meanwhile, a person from Kuala Lumpur won the remaining jackpot, totalling RM1,975,853.50.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Man charged with elderly neighbour's murder in Taman Desa Aman
Article:
IPOH: An unemployed man was charged in the magistrate's court here today with the
murder of his elderly neighbour
last week.
Foon Choon Keong, 53, nodded after the charge was read before Magistrate Evangelin Simon Silvaraj, but no plea was recorded as the case falls under the jurisdiction of the High Court.
According to the charge, Foon, who is unmarried, is accused of
murdering Woo Chui Fun
, 70, at a house in Persiaran Pegoh Aman 2, Taman Desa Aman, between 4.40pm and 10pm on May 4, 2026.
The charge, framed under Section 302 of the Penal Code, carries the death penalty or a jail term of between 30 and 40 years, and a minimum of 12 strokes of the rotan, upon conviction.
Prosecution was led by deputy public prosecutor Muhammad Zulqarnain Wan Asmira, while lawyer Khesminkapil Singh Dhanoa represented the accused.
No bail was granted, and the court set Aug 3 for mention for the submission of the chemist and autopsy reports.– BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Hantavirus outbreak renews painful memories for Patagonian village
Article:
MAILEN Valle lost her father and two sisters during a hantavirus outbreak more than seven years ago in Epuyen, a village in Argentina's Patagonia region.
With the recent hantavirus outbreak on the MV Hondius cruise ship, hard memories have resurfaced for the 33-year-old.
"Losing my dad and my two sisters in less than a month..." she told AFP, trailing off.
Her voice broke and she laughed nervously, opting to read from a prepared statement because she knew it would be hard to speak.
"Nobody was prepared to see how, in a matter of days, a family table was left empty," she said.
While the Hondius outbreak has left three people dead, it has yet to surpass the Epuyen outbreak, which recorded 34 cases and 11 fatalities between December 2018 and March 2019 in the town of 2,400 residents, situated in a part of the Andes where hantavirus is endemic.
[[nid:1437119]]
Mailen's father, Aldo Valle, came down with it after attending a birthday party.
"The person with the virus was just sitting at the same table as my dad. And at that table there were several people who got infected, and people died," Mailen recalled.
The wake for Valle was another locus of infection, where all three of his daughters got sick.
One sister died "within hours" of showing symptoms, while for the other, "we had to take her to the cemetery without a wake," Mailen said.
The variant of the hantavirus suspected in both outbreaks is transmitted through the droppings, saliva and urine of the Andean mouse.
Jorge Diaz, an epidemiologist with Chubut province's health department who worked on the Epuyen hantavirus outbreak, told AFP that "we knew very little about the disease" in 2018.
Human-to-human transmission of hantavirus was first discovered in 1996 in the neighbouring town of El Bolson, and it was later found to have happened in Epuyen.
"We implemented quarantine, which required those who made contact with a positive case to isolate for 45 days," Diaz explained.
About 100 people ended up undergoing the quarantine process in a display that would foreshadow the Covid-19 pandemic that broke out a year later.
The approach, dubbed "selective isolation," marked a shift in the epidemiological response, and now "each time there is a case of (Andes) hantavirus, isolation is ordered or recommended."
Residents in Patagonia know how to protect themselves from the virus, which they refer to as "the hanta," by airing out sheds and cleaning areas with bleach.
[[nid:1437118]]
But the human transmission of the Epuyen outbreak changed the scale of the fight, as one could get infected from their neighbour just as easily as from an Andean mouse.
Mailen remembers the stigma. "We felt very discriminated against," she said.
Others recall being banned from shops in nearby towns.
Isabel Diaz, 53, survived the outbreak with a different stigma – her father, Victor Diaz, was labelled "patient zero," and attended the birthday party while displaying the early symptoms of hantavirus.
"People looked poorly at my father. It's not his fault he got sick," she told AFP, her eyes welling up.
"Nobody chooses to get sick, much less infect others, much less lose a mother."
Isabel got sick from her father's hantavirus case, as well as her mother. "She was the sixth patient" of the eleven who died, she said.
Her father, for his part, recalled how it felt to come down with hantavirus, causing body aches and a bitter taste that even made sipping water unpleasant.
"It started with a feeling of weakness. I didn't feel like eating. And I started to get purple spots," he said. "That same day, I lost consciousness."
In the years since the hantavirus outbreak, Epuyen has endured the Covid-19 pandemic and major wildfires in 2025 and 2026, permanently changing the landscape.
"It's one thing after another," Victor said, laughing.
"No one is going to tell us what it means to live life and keep moving forward," Isabel Diaz added.
* The writer is from AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bank Negara, Bank Indonesia sign MoU to strengthen bilateral cooperation
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bank Negara Malaysia and Bank Indonesia (BI) have signed a new memorandum of understanding (MoU) that strengthens bilateral cooperation between the two central banks.
In a joint statement today, they said that the MoU provides a comprehensive platform to guide cooperation in a range of central banking areas.
This includes monetary policy, financial stability and macroprudential policy, payment systems and digitalisation, financial sector development, capacity-building initiatives, and information sharing, they said.
Bank Negara governor Datuk Seri Abdul Rasheed Ghaffour said the MoU reaffirms the longstanding partnership between Bank Negara and BI.
"It also underscores our shared commitment to deepen collaboration across established areas of central banking, while expanding cooperation into emerging areas of mutual interest," he said.
BI governor Perry Warjiyo said the MoU is a significant milestone in advancing the long-standing relationship between BI and Bank Negara, and more than a symbolic commitment.
"It is a testament to the enduring spirit of strengthening institutional ties and deepening bilateral cooperation between the two central banks, especially in current challenging geopolitical landscape," he said.
According to the statement, the signing of the MoU sets the stage for a more robust and synergistic partnership between Bank Negara and BI, and both central banks look forward to continued cooperation in the mutually agreed areas for the greater prosperity of both economies.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Ah longs hurl Molotov cocktails at JB homes, four families living in fear
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Four families in Bukit Indah are living in fear after their homes along Jalan Indah were hit by Molotov cocktail attacks over two consecutive days.
Perling assemblyman Liew Chin Tong said the attacks, which occurred last Friday and Saturday, are believed to be the work of an unlicensed moneylending syndicate based in Singapore.
He said the petrol bombs were targeted at one house, but the fire spread to three neighbouring properties.
Aside from damage to the front of the houses, several cars belonging to the victims were destroyed in the fire.
Liew said police are investigating the case and have increased patrols in the area.
Police have confirmed that the probe is being carried out under Section 5(2) of the Moneylenders Act for unlicensed moneylending and Section 435 of the Penal Code for mischief by fire.
The offences are punishable by lengthy jail terms and fines.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Nepalese worker jailed three months for breaking colleague's arm with iron rod
Article:
BATU PAHAT: A Nepalese factory worker who broke a colleague's arm with an iron rod during a dispute over a broom was sentenced to three months' jail by the Magistrate's Court here today.
Bikash Giri, 30, pleaded guilty before Magistrate Arun Noval Dass to a charge under Section 325 of the Penal Code for voluntarily causing grievous hurt.
He was charged with assaulting the 47-year-old victim at a workers' hostel in Taman Megah at 6.30pm on April 8.
The offence carries a maximum penalty of seven years' imprisonment and a fine.
According to the facts of the case, an employee of a cleaning company received a report of a fight between two Nepalese workers at the hostel.
Giri had struck the victim on the right arm and the back of the head with an iron rod.
The assault left the victim with a broken right arm, while Giri required five stitches to his head following the altercation.
Police arrested Giri at 1.30pm the following day, where he led them to the 24cm iron rod used in the attack.
The altercation reportedly stemmed from the victim scolding one of Giri's friends for sweeping the hostel room, which had disturbed the victim's sleep.
Deputy public prosecutor Firdaus Ruslan prosecuted, while the accused was unrepresented.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Cambodian man missing after immigration raid found drowned in Sungai Pahang
Article:
PEKAN: A Cambodian man who went missing after his wife and six children were detained in an Immigration Department raid was found drowned in Sungai Pahang here yesterday.
The body of the 51-year-old from Kampung Pulau Keladi was found floating near the Pekan Riverfront about 8.30am. He is believed to have fallen into the river.
Pekan district police chief Superintendent Mohd Zaidi Mat Zin said the victim's younger brother lodged a police report about 5pm on May 9 after failing to locate him.
"He believed his brother had fallen into Sungai Pahang and drowned. A search and rescue operation involving the police, Fire and Rescue Department and Civil Defence Force was launched near the victim's house.
"The body was found about 8.30am yesterday and sent to Pekan Hospital for a post-mortem. Investigations found no criminal elements," he said in a statement today.
Zaidi said checks with the Pahang Immigration Department found only the victim's wife and six children were in custody following the raid, while the victim was missing.
"This prompted the victim's brother to lodge a police report.
"However, no witnesses saw the victim entering the river. The case has been classified as drowning," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia's volume index of services up by 5.8pct in Q1 FY26: Statistics Department
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's volume index of services recorded a year-on-year growth of 5.8 per cent in the first quarter (1Q) of 2026, reaching 168.0 points, according to the Statistics Department.
The index covers four segments, namely wholesale and retail trade, food and beverage and accommodation; business services and finance; information and communication and transportation and storage; and other services, it said.
In a statement today, chief statistician Malaysia Datuk Seri Dr Mohd Uzir Mahidin said that overall, growth in the volume index of services in the 1Q 2026 was mainly driven by the wholesale and retail trade, food and beverage, and accommodation segment, which expanded by 5.7 per cent.
"This was followed by the information and communication and transportation and storage segment, which recorded an increase of 8.1 per cent.
"Meanwhile, the business services and finance segment grew by 3.0 per cent, while the other services segment registered a higher growth of 9.3 per cent," he said.
The department said that the volume index for the wholesale and retail trade, food and beverage and accommodation segment rose by 5.7 per cent to 166.3 points, supported by growth in wholesale and retail trade (4.9 per cent), food and beverage (8.5 per cent) and accommodation (15.0 per cent).
"At the same time, the information and communication and transportation and storage segment increased by 8.1 per cent to 191.2 points, driven by growth in information and communication (8.0 per cent) and transportation and storage (8.3 per cent)," it said.
Meanwhile, the department said that the business services and finance segment recorded a volume index of 156.8 points, with a growth of 3.0 per cent.
"This increase was supported by professional and administrative and support services (6.2 per cent), followed by real estate (7.4 per cent) and finance and insurance (0.1 per cent), reflecting steady business activity and supportive market conditions during the quarter," it said.
Additionally, the department said that the other services segment grew by 9.3 per cent to 146.9 points, driven by increases in arts, entertainment and recreation and personal services and other activities (8.8 per cent), private health (9.7 per cent), and private education (9.3 per cent).
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Family proud Singaporean hiker died trying to save others in Mount Dukono eruption
Article:
GALELA: The family of Singaporean hiker Timothy Heng, who died during the Mount Dukono volcano eruption in North Halmahera, Indonesia, said they were proud he spent his final moments trying to help others.
According to The Straits Times, the 30-year-old was among two Singaporeans killed after the active volcano erupted on May 8, sending hot rocks and ash raining down on a group of hikers near the summit.
His step-sister, Tessa Oh, said the family had clung to hope for two days that he was still alive somewhere on the mountain before receiving devastating news from Singapore's Ministry of Foreign Affairs (MFA) on May 10 that two bodies had been found near the summit.
"From the way that MFA communicated, they did not exactly confirm that he was dead, but from the wording, we kind of assumed that was the case," said Oh, a journalist with The Business Times, as reported by The Straits Times.
She said her parents and Heng's soon-to-be fiancée were especially devastated as they had hoped he would be found alive.
Heng was part of a group of 20 hikers — nine Singaporeans and 11 Indonesians — who trekked up the volcano on May 7.
Seventeen hikers were evacuated safely, while three people — two Singaporeans and one Indonesian — died in the eruption.
Oh said her first thought after learning more details was that her brother had likely gone back to save someone.
"That is the Tim we know. It just sounded so much like what he would do," she said.
She stressed that Heng did not die because he took unnecessary risks, but because he was trying to help others.
That belief was reinforced after she read The Straits Times' interview with Indonesian guide Reza Selang, who said Heng could have escaped with the rest of the Singaporean climbers, but instead chose to return up the mountain to help another member of the group.
"Reza's own testimony is that he could have escaped with the rest of the Singaporeans who evacuated, but he went back up.
"He showed bravery, he really tried to help, and he really thought of all the participants before himself," Oh said.
She added that the family was proud of his courage and the values he lived by.
Heng was also the organiser of the expedition, having planned the trip nearly a year earlier through a travel community he ran in Singapore.
The group went on the trek despite a climbing ban imposed by local authorities on April 17 and a standing 4km exclusion zone around the crater.
Guide Reza reportedly said he was unaware of the warnings.
Oh said she had seen negative comments online questioning why the group chose to climb Mount Dukono despite the risks and hoped people would show compassion.
"We are obviously super sad and really grieving the loss of our brother and our son. But knowing that his last moments were spent trying to help others just makes us proud," she said.
She said the family was now working to bring Heng's body home and expressed gratitude to Indonesian search-and-rescue teams for their efforts.
The other Singaporean victim was identified as 27-year-old Shahin Muhrez Abdul Hamid.
His mother, teacher Noraini Ibrahim, told Berita Harian that learning of his death felt like
"a wall collapsing"
, describing her youngest son as someone who always put family first and was "irreplaceable".
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Gamuda unit begins work on Australia's Jinbi solar farm
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Gamuda Bhd's Australian subsidiary, DT Infrastructure, has begun work on the Jinbi Solar Farm for Yindjibarndi Energy Corpn (YEC), a flagship 75-megawatt renewable energy project in Western Australia's Pilbara region.
Located in Yindjibarndi Ngurra, about 56 kilometres south of Karratha, the project will feature around 160,000 solar panels and is expected to play a key role in accelerating the region's shift towards cleaner energy.
Once completed, the Jinbi Solar Farm will supply renewable electricity to Rio Tinto's regional power network, helping to support mining operations with a more sustainable and reliable energy source while reducing carbon emissions in the Pilbara.
In a statement, DT Infrastructure chief executive John Anderson said the project marks an important step for both the company's growing renewable energy portfolio and the broader energy transition in Western Australia.
"This project is not only a vital addition to our renewable energy pipeline but also a testament to our commitment to the Pilbara's future infrastructure.
"Partnering with one of Australia's largest Indigenous-led renewable energy initiatives allows us to support YEC in delivering meaningful, long-term outcomes for the Yindjibarndi community," he said.
The Jinbi Solar Farm forms part of YEC's wider renewable energy development plans across its Chichester Range and Eastern Development Zone hubs, which collectively support more than 1.5 gigawatts (GW) of proposed wind, solar and battery storage capacity in the Pilbara.
YEC chief executive officer Craig Ricato said DT Infrastructure's involvement is a key step in realising the company's vision for sustainable energy on Yindjibarndi.
Established in 2023, YEC aims to develop up to 3GW of renewable energy capacity across roughly 13,000 square kilometres of Yindjibarndi Ngurra, supporting decarbonisation, electrification and the development of new industries in the region, while generating long-term economic and social benefits for the Yindjibarndi people.
Project mobilisation is already underway, with completion targeted for early 2028.
DT Infrastructure continues to position itself as a key delivery partner in Australia's clean energy transition, focusing on large-scale infrastructure projects that support long-term sustainability goals.
Gamuda has been steadily expanding its renewable energy footprint across the Asia Pacific, spanning solar, hydro and wind developments.
Its portfolio under DT Infrastructure includes projects such as Stage 1 of Marinus Link, the Carmody's Hill Wind Farm, the Goulburn River Solar Farm, and the Boulder Creek Wind Farm.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MIDF approves Malaysia's first IP-backed financing facility
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysian Industrial Development Finance Bhd (MIDF) has approved its first financing facility under the World Intellectual Property Organisation–MIDF Intellectual Property (IP)-Backed Financing Pilot Programme.
The first recipient, a Malaysian small and medium enterprise, obtained the facility by using its intellectual property assets as a key part of the credit assessment and security package.
MIDF, a unit of MBSB Bhd, said the milestone marks a significant shift in Malaysia's financial landscape, with intellectual property now recognised as a form of collateral and security interest.
"The transaction utilised specialised methodologies for IP identification, valuation, and risk assessment developed under the pilot framework," it said in a statement.
The programme is further supported by a RM10 million fund from SME Corp, which is specifically allocated to facilitate IP-backed financing.
MIDF chief executive officer Azizi Mustafa said the milestone marks a turning point in the financial landscape, demonstrating that intellectual property can serve as a viable and powerful form of collateral.
He added that by unlocking the value of intangible assets, MIDF is enabling innovation-driven SMEs to turn their ideas and creativity into scalable business growth.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 13 senators sworn in: Six new appointees, seven reappointed
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Thirteen members of the Senate, including six new appointees, took their oath of office today before the Senate president, Datuk Awang Bemee Awang Ali Basah.
The six new appointees are acting Bentong Umno chief Datuk Seri Mohamad Zaini Salleh, Wanita MCA chief Datuk Wong You Fong, Sabah DAP chairman Datuk Phoong Jin Zhe, Johor DAP vice-chairman Sheikh Umar Bagharib Ali, Sarawak DAP assistant secretary Larry Asap and R.Thiagarajah.
Meanwhile, six senators were reappointed, namely Tan Sri Low Kian Chuan, Datuk Seri Profesor Dr Awang Sariyan, Datuk Dr Mohd Hatta Md Ramli, Isaiah D. Jacob, Mohd Hasbie Muda and Abun Sui Anyitm.
Abdul Nasir Idris was re-elected by the Kedah State Assembly.
All six new appointments by the King are for a three-year term from today until May 10, 2029, while the reappointments run from March 20, 2026, to March 19, 2029.
In his speech, Awang Bemee congratulated the 13 senators and told them to make the Senate a platform for mature and constructive debate in representing the voices of the states and federal territories.
The Senate president also noted that the world is currently facing challenging geopolitical and economic conditions, including global supply chain disruptions, energy market uncertainty, rising logistics costs and inflationary pressures affecting the cost of living.
"Malaysia's parliament is entering an important phase through the enforcement of the Parliamentary Services Act to strengthen the separation of powers.
"The Senate also approved amendments to its standing orders on March 9, including the establishment of five new select committees covering public administration and governance; trade, industry and international relations; food security and the economy; social services, minorities, culture and indigenous affairs; as well as defence and security," he said.
Awang Bemee told senators to play an active role in the committees to strengthen the Senate institution and champion public interests.
He said that in today's digital communication era, senators' responsibilities extend beyond the chamber and include fostering public understanding of constitutional supremacy, parliamentary democracy and the rule of law.
"Use social media and various communication platforms responsibly to assist the public, strengthen unity and preserve national harmony," he added. — BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Police arrest Proton Inspira driver after viral three-vehicle crash on NKVE
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, May 11 — Police have arrested a 26-year-old Proton Inspira driver following a three-vehicle collision on the New Klang Valley Expressway (NKVE) that went viral on social media, according to Free Malaysia Today (FMT).
Kuala Lumpur Traffic Investigation and Enforcement Department chief ACP Mohd Zamzuri Mohd Isa said the accident occurred at about 11pm on May 9 at Km28 of the highway heading towards the Duta toll plaza.
He said the Proton Inspira had skidded and stopped in the fast lane before it was hit by a Proton Wira and a Toyota Hilux.
“The impact caused the vehicles to veer into the left road barrier, resulting in heavy damage to all three vehicles,” he said in a statement cited by FMT.
No injuries were reported among the drivers involved.
Zamzuri said the case is being investigated under Section 42(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987 for reckless driving.
“Actions taken include obtaining CCTV footage from the scene, recording witness statements, visiting the scene, and inspecting and photographing the vehicles involved,” he was quoted as saying.

Title: Penang DAP Kampung Kolam branch first to declare Anthony Loke welcome to contest in the state
Article:
GEORGE TOWN, May 11 — The Kampung Kolam DAP branch has passed a resolution to welcome and support party secretary-general Anthony Loke to contest in Penang, marking the first time a branch in the state has openly made such a call.
According to
The Star,
the resolution was passed unanimously during the branch’s annual general meeting yesterday. It was proposed by former Komtar assemblyman and Tanjong MP Ng Wei Aik, who was elected as the branch’s new chairman, and seconded by branch secretary Khoo Yeong Ming.
Ng said the resolution was made with the aspiration of leading Penang towards greater progress. He noted that while Penang has always been a frontline state for DAP, which has never abandoned its goal of making the state a political fortress, the current state leadership is increasingly plagued by issues such as uneven leadership quality, factional infighting, and money-driven political campaigns.
Ng believes the time has come to invite Loke to lead the state chapter, which he said would strengthen the party’s electoral prospects and drive Penang’s development to greater heights.
At the AGM, other resolutions were also passed such as prioritising party members with a Tanjong background as candidates for parliamentary and state seats in the area, urging the party leadership to contest at least 21 state seats in the state polls, as well as a new policy limiting party members from contesting any elected position for more than three terms consecutively.

Title: Bangsar shooting case: Lorry driver charged with attempted murder
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, May 11 — A lorry driver pleaded not guilty in the Sessions Court here today to a charge of attempting to murder a man during a shooting incident outside an entertainment centre in Bangsar last month.
A. Lekeswaran, 27, was charged with attempting to kill scrap metal business owner B. Yoga Sundram, 41, in front of an entertainment outlet along Jalan Telawi, Bangsar, at about 3.15 am on April 22.
The charge, framed under Section 307 of the Penal Code, carries a maximum prison sentence of 10 years and a fine upon conviction. If the act causes injury, the accused may face up to 20 years’ imprisonment.
Deputy public prosecutor J. Banusha objected to bail, arguing that the offence was serious and noting that the victim remains in critical condition in the Intensive Care Unit of Universiti Malaya Medical Centre.
“Granting bail is inappropriate because it may allow the accused to flee, and there is also the possibility of interference with the victim and prosecution witnesses.
“It is also understood that the accused is expected to face firearms-related charges in the Klang Court. Therefore, I object to the bail application and request the court not to grant bail,” he said.
Counsel Muhammad Izz Khan Mohd Nor Khan, representing the accused, appealed for bail and informed the court that his client was willing to comply with any additional conditions imposed.
However, Judge Mazuliana Abdul Rashid denied bail and fixed June 10 for mention. — Bernama

Title: Six new senators sworn-in
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, May 11 —Thirteen members of the Senate, including six new appointees, took their oath of office today before the Senate president, Datuk Awang Bemee Awang Ali Basah.
The six new appointees are acting Bentong Umno chief Datuk Seri Mohamad Zaini Salleh, Wanita MCA chief Datuk Wong You Fong, Sabah DAP chairman Datuk Phoong Jin Zhe, Johor DAP vice-chairman Sheikh Umar Bagharib Ali, Sarawak DAP assistant secretary Larry Asap and R.Thiagarajah.
Meanwhile, six senators were reappointed, namely Tan Sri Low Kian Chuan, Datuk Seri Profesor Dr Awang Sariyan, Datuk Dr Mohd Hatta Md Ramli, Isaiah D. Jacob, Mohd Hasbie Muda and Abun Sui Anyitm.
Abdul Nasir Idris was re-elected by the Kedah State Legislative Assembly.
All six new appointments by the Yang di-Pertuan Agong are for a three-year term from today until May 10, 2029, while the reappointments run from March 20, 2026, to March 19, 2029.
In his speech, Awang Bemee congratulated the 13 senators and urged them to make the Senate a platform for mature and constructive debate in representing the voices of the states and federal territories.
The Senate president also noted that the world is currently facing challenging geopolitical and economic conditions, including global supply chain disruptions, energy market uncertainty, rising logistics costs and inflationary pressures affecting the cost of living.
"Malaysia’s Parliament is entering an important phase through the enforcement of the Parliamentary Services Act 2025 to strengthen the separation of powers.
"The Senate also approved amendments to its standing orders on March 9, including the establishment of five new select committees covering public administration and governance; trade, industry and international relations; food security and the economy; social services, minorities, culture and indigenous affairs; as well as defence and security,” he said.
As such, Awang Bemee urged senators to play an active role in the committees to strengthen the Senate institution and champion public interests.
He said that in today’s digital communication era, senators’ responsibilities extend beyond the chamber and include fostering public understanding of constitutional supremacy, parliamentary democracy and the rule of law.
"Use social media and various communication platforms responsibly to assist the public, strengthen unity and preserve national harmony,” he added. — Bernama

Title: Penang opposition leader alleges Chief Minister Incorporated accounts not fully audited
Article:
GEORGE TOWN, May 11 – Penang Opposition Leader Muhammad Fauzi Yusoff has alleged that the accounts of government-linked corporations, Chief Minister Incorporated (CMI), was not fully audited.
He said CMI had failed to present its complete and audited accounts.
“The accounts were also not tabled in the state legislative assembly even though CMI manages the state revenue and multi-million ringgit projects,” he said when debating the Penang Yang DiPertua Negri’s speech at the state legislative assembly today.
The Sungai Dua assemblyman questioned the transparency of the state’s revenue management under CMI.
“How can an entity managing millions in state revenue operate without proper financial transparency? This is not about perception, this is about basic governance principles,” he said.
He said CMI which oversees various projects, should have a clear and audited financial reporting mechanism.
“CMI managed 71 projects in 2024 and 2025 and recorded rental and lease income through CMI and State Secretary Incorporated (SSI) amounting to RM8.43 million as of September 30 last year,” he said.
He also questioned delays in the tabling of CMI’s financial statements, despite requirements under amendments to the Chief Minister Incorporated Enactment, which stipulate that accounts and records must be properly prepared and tabled in the State Assembly every financial year.
Fauzi raised concerns over CMI’s management structure.
He said it lacked transparency especially in relation to state land management, strategic leases, and collaborations through various special purpose vehicles (SPVs).
“The public still does not have a clear picture of all companies under CMI’s management, their shareholding structures, and the actual ownership links,” he said.
He added that this raises questions over who is managing state assets and what benefits are derived from the projects, while also limiting public scrutiny over financial flows.
He urged the state government to explain the issues he raised.
“I hope the state government ensure all CMI-related accounts are properly prepared, audited, and presented openly to the state assembly and the public,” he said.

Title: Penang tightens entry-point health security despite low hantavirus risk in Malaysia
Article:
GEORGE TOWN, May 11 — Penang has strengthened health security at its international entry points at both the port and international airport to prevent the spread of hantavirus here.
State exco for health, youth and sports Daniel Gooi Zi Sen said steps have been taken to tighten international entry screening measures.
“These steps include increasing the capacity of existing laboratories to assist in detecting potential hantavirus infections,” he said in a press conference at the state legislative assembly today.
He reiterated a statement by the Ministry of Health that at present hantavirus cases occurred mainly in European countries so the likelihood of transmission in Malaysia is considered relatively low.
However the state health department has continued to implement health security protocols.
“We have health officers stationed at international entry points including at the ports,” he said.
He said ships arriving at Penang’s ports are subject to specific protocols to inspect their cleanliness and related health conditions.
“For the information of the media, according to the, from January 1 to May 2 this year, a total of 22,367 ships and boats were inspected at international entry points nationwide across major ports,” he said.
During the same period, Gooi said 2,421 sanitary certificates and 1,146 Ship Sanitation Certificates (SSC) were issued to ensure vessels entering the country comply with public health requirements, sanitation standards, and pest control measures in line with international health regulations.
“These are existing public health protocols already in place, implemented under the Ministry of Health to prevent the spread of infectious diseases,” he said.
He said these protocols have been in place for a long time.
Among the common measures include the use of infrared cameras to detect if a person has a fever or related symptoms.
“This is part of the established protocol to prevent disease transmission,” he said.
He said health officers at all international entry points have been reminded to remain alert to symptoms associated with the virus.
.

Title: Sabah revises tourism strategy after airline route suspensions affect Kota Kinabalu connectivity
Article:
KOTA KINABALU, May 11 — Sabah is reviewing its tourism targets and adopting a more flexible strategy following a series of airline route suspensions affecting connectivity into the state capital.
State Tourism, Culture and Environment Minister Datuk Jafry Ariffin said tourism planning would now be guided by a more adaptive framework as global aviation conditions continue to shift amid geopolitical tensions, rising operational costs and changes in airline capacity.
“We are already seeing these changes impacting airline capacity, with route suspensions and reduced frequencies affecting connectivity into Kota Kinabalu, and we are closely monitoring developments to ensure our tourism planning remains responsive,” he said in a statement.
Among the affected routes are AirAsia’s temporary suspension of its Singapore–Kota Kinabalu route from May 13 to November 30, Jakarta–Kota Kinabalu from April 16 to May 21, and Ho Chi Minh–Kota Kinabalu beginning July 1.
Batik Air has also suspended its Seoul–Kota Kinabalu service effective April 28, while AirAsia will temporarily halt its Sibu–Kota Kinabalu and Bintulu–Kota Kinabalu routes between June 8 and June 30.
The suspensions come as Sabah continues to rely heavily on direct air connectivity to drive tourism growth, particularly from regional and international markets.
Jafry said the developments reflect broader global pressures that may influence visitor arrivals and travel demand.
He said tourism performance planning is now based on multiple scenarios, including prolonged external disruptions, stabilising market conditions and possible recovery of airline capacity.
“As at March, arrivals are still on track towards the original target of four million arrivals, but targets may be revised depending on real-time developments in airline connectivity and market conditions,” he said.
Based on preliminary figures, Sabah recorded 932,970 visitor arrivals up to March this year, comprising 537,317 domestic visitors and 395,653 international arrivals.
Jafry said the state government and the Sabah Tourism Board (STB) are continuing to engage airline partners and industry stakeholders to strengthen connectivity and minimise disruptions to visitor flows.
“This approach is supported by continuous market intelligence, research and industry engagement led by STB, including coordination with airline partners to strengthen connectivity planning and support decision-making on tourism strategy,” he said.
Despite the challenges, he said Sabah remains committed to sustaining tourism growth by intensifying promotions in domestic and regional markets with existing flight connections.
“In the current environment, we must remain adaptive and responsive,” he said.

Title: Hit-and-run at Borneo Marathon leaves runner, Rela personnel injured; organisers say police probing incident
Article:
KOTA KINABALU, May 11 — Organisers of the Borneo International Marathon 2026 have confirmed a hit-and-run incident during the race, which left a 40-year-old participant and a Rela personnel injured.
In a statement today, Kinabalu Running Club said both victims received immediate treatment from the event’s medical team before being taken to hospital for further observation and care.
“We have also contacted the victims’ families and are closely monitoring their condition while extending support to those affected and their loved ones,” the organisers said.
The runner, a full marathon participant in the 42km category, was believed to have sustained a neck fracture during the incident, which occurred in the early hours of the morning while it was still dark.
The Rela personnel involved is 37 years old.
The club said the matter has been reported to the police and investigations are ongoing.
The organisers also reminded motorists to respect road closures and safety measures during organised sporting events.
“The safety of runners, volunteers, officials and all event personnel remains our highest priority. We urge all road users to respect road closures, marathon safety measures, and the presence of runners and crews during organised sporting events,” they said.
They said they had engaged traffic management consultant Danasafe for the race.
However, following the incident, several runners took to social media to complain about inadequate road safety along certain stretches, including areas that were reportedly in darkness or not fully closed to traffic.
In a separate incident, a half-marathon runner who completed the race later died at hospital after experiencing shortness of breath.
Muhd Irfan Tarmizi Muhlis @ Ramli, a lifeguard at the Tawau Sports Complex, reportedly completed the 21km category before developing symptoms.
The Borneo International Marathon is in its 16th edition and attracts more than 10,000 runners from 50 countries.

Title: Customs seizes illicit cash, drugs and liquor at KLIA
Article:
SEPANG, May 11 — Customs officers at the Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) have foiled attempts to smuggle out cash and drugs, and seized smuggled liquor, with a total value of nearly RM1.12 million in four separate cases last month.
KLIA Customs director Zulkifli Muhammad said that in the first case on April 2, the department, in collaboration with the Aviation Security (AVSEC) unit of Malaysia Airports, detained an Indonesian traveller at the Satellite Building, Terminal 1, for allegedly attempting to take RM129,500 in cash out of the country without declaration.
“A search of the traveller’s luggage uncovered the cash, which had been concealed inside a black plastic bag. The traveller failed to declare the cash in Customs Form No. 7 for the amount to be taken out of Malaysia and breached Regulation 55(1)(b) of the Customs Regulations 2019,” he said in a statement today.
He said the traveller was supposed to obtain prior written approval from Bank Negara Malaysia before taking the money out of the country.
The case is being investigated under Section 28B of the Anti-Money Laundering, Anti-Terrorism Financing and Proceeds of Unlawful Activities Act 2001.
He said that in the second case, officers raided a premises in Seri Kembangan at 7.15 pm on April 23 and discovered 17.043 litres of various brands of alcoholic beverages, valued at an estimated RM221,566, with unpaid duties amounting to RM430,835.
“The case is being investigated under Section 135(1)(d) of the Customs Act 1967 and Section 74(1)(d) of the Excise Act 1976,” he said.
In the third and fourth cases on April 24 and 27 at the international departure gate of KLIA Terminal 1, Zulkifli said three local individuals were detained for allegedly attempting to smuggle drugs out of the country.
He said that at 9.20 am on April 24, officers from the KLIA Customs Enforcement Division, in collaboration with AVSEC Malaysia Airports KLIA, detained a local man who was due to board a flight to Hong Kong after X-ray screening of his luggage revealed suspicious images believed to be drugs.
“A further inspection of the luggage uncovered ketamine weighing 3.6719 kilogrammes, valued at RM183,595. The case has been handed over to the KLIA Enforcement Division for further investigation,” he said.
Zulkifli said that at 5.17 pm on April 27, two local women, who were due to board a flight to Taipei, were detained after X-ray screening of their luggage also revealed suspicious images believed to be drugs.
